mnemonics: SWING: See What I Need, Guys! (Swing provides Widgets, Is lightweight, Natively cross-platform, and uses a Graphics-based GUI system)
keyPoints: Swing is a lightweight, platform-independent GUI widget toolkit built on top of AWT.
It uses the Model-View-Controller (MVC) architecture for flexible and powerful UI components.
Swing components are painted directly onto the canvas using Java code, making them more consistent across platforms than heavy-weight AWT peers.
commonMistakes: Mixing AWT (heavyweight) and Swing (lightweight) components in the same container, which can cause rendering issues and z-order problems.
Performing long-running tasks on the Event Dispatch Thread (EDT), which freezes the GUI and creates a poor user experience.
